$schema_note: V6-3 human-gold query set (V6-20 eval), pre-registered before the V6-2 graph is built (FABLE R4). AI-drafted v0, pending operator spot-check — see README.md.

entries.query: What is the #25 external-thinking re-audit gate, and when does it 'land' — at the pre-audit or the re-audit?
entries.rationale: design-doc.md's own § Re-audit gate states verbatim: '#25 is a standing re-audit gate... it lands when the re-audit... runs, not the pre-audit.'

entries.query: What is FABLE-PRE-VERDICT and why was it written ahead of the re-audit rather than during it?
entries.rationale: The memo's own header states it was rendered 2026-07-06 because the operator's frontier-model access was about to lapse before Wave E staged.

entries.query: Why is GBrain's published BrainBench benchmark (P@5 49.1% / R@5 97.9%) not trusted at face value?
entries.rationale: Both docs state the numbers are self-reported, N=1, on a 240-page synthetic Opus-generated corpus with self-derived queries and only an internal ablation — 'replicate the method, discard the figures.'

entries.query: What verb vocabulary does agentm's V6-2 knowledge graph use for typed edges, and where does it come from?
entries.rationale: design-doc.md's V6-2 synthesis names the verbs seeded from the gist (uses, depends-on, contradicts, caused, fixed, supersedes) plus agentm's own (decided-in, implements, references) — not GBrain's VC-shaped vocabulary.

entries.query: Is multi-hop graph traversal fused into V6-3's RRF retrieval at launch?
entries.rationale: FABLE R2 rules it ships as a separate V6-19 browse/discovery verb, not a third RRF stream, until edge precision is measured and clears a floor.

entries.query: What is the governing safety constraint that every storage/backend design in the V5 arc must not violate?
entries.rationale: Both cite 'never-orphan-the-vault' (+ never-orphan-the-operator, vault-as-canonical-context) as the governing safety constraint, repeated verbatim across the V5 storage design family.

entries.query: What does V5-3 (storage cutover) actually delete, rename, and lock, relative to V5-1's seam?
entries.rationale: The design-doc states plainly: 'V5-3 deletes the built-in, renames the vault, and locks the index-safety invariant,' building on V5-1's seam and V5-2's backing plugin.

entries.query: What's the difference between backing the vault with vault-git versus vault-drive, and are they mutually exclusive?
entries.rationale: vault-git.md states 'Mutually exclusive with vault-drive — one transport per folder'; vault-drive.md frames Drive as discouraged/invisible to tooling versus git's tooled propose-via-PR path.

entries.query: What does the autonomous-write contract's A3 scope actually cover, and what resolves it?
entries.rationale: Direct source of the A3 scoped-to-personal-notes autonomous-write resolution, cited repeatedly across the audit and MCP-server designs as the real write-authz boundary.

entries.query: What did the MemoryOS/Tencent architecture scan conclude about heat-based tiered memory, and was it adopted?
entries.rationale: The scan explicitly lists heat-based tiered-memory as 'Deliberately not adopted' — over-engineering for a solo-operator vault — with only the floor-policy half kept at #46.

entries.query: What is the LLM Wiki v2 gist's source-watch baseline, and what would trigger a fresh look at it?
entries.rationale: The doc names the 2026-06-05 snapshot as the diff anchor and explicitly names two re-audit triggers: a new top-level technique cluster, or a concrete confidence-decay/crystallization-trigger algorithm.

entries.query: Why was the V5-11 PM chief-of-staff design pulled forward, and what does it depend on?
entries.rationale: V5-11's design-doc names V5-10's coordinator-team foundation as the thing it 'can't ship before.'

entries.query: What does the V5 acceleration plan assume must already be in place before it applies?
entries.rationale: Its opening line states it 'assumes the coordinator-directed team work (building now) is in place' — i.e. V5-10.

entries.query: What is the mechanical uplift measurement R3 shipped, and is it a merge gate?
entries.rationale: The archived R3 plan states explicitly it 'is not a merge gate — the mechanical rows are always-on reporting inside the existing deterministic tier.'

entries.query: Why was the live-uplift probe (Tasks 3-4 of R3) built and then reverted the same day?
entries.rationale: The plan's own Task 4 status note + Locked design calls record an operator mid-plan descope call, with checkers.py removed as designed-but-unconsumed code.

entries.query: What are the four ABLATE_* flags R3 added, and what does each simulate?
entries.rationale: Task 1's Status note lists ABLATE_VECTORS / ABLATE_EVIDENCE_TRACKER / ABLATE_REFLECTION / ABLATE_GATES verbatim with what each ablates.

entries.query: What is the sequencing gate that had to be satisfied before Wave E (V6) could open?
entries.rationale: PLAN-wave-e-v6-index.md's own header states 'R3 -> Wave E / V6 (gated on the eval harness R builds). Satisfied - R3 shipped and archived 2026-07-05.'

entries.query: What edge-extraction property has never been measured by any source discussing GBrain's knowledge graph, and why does that matter?
entries.rationale: design-doc.md's V6-2 row states explicitly: 'no source measures edge-extraction precision/recall — only downstream retrieval... make edge-P/R a first-class V6-20 metric.'

entries.query: Why does the design decline to promote the gist's numeric confidence-decay score to V6-1's lifecycle state?
entries.rationale: The 'V6-1 - why ADAPT, not ADOPT' subsection: no source actually parameterizes the illustrative 0.85 constant, and link-chains carry supersession more honestly than an unparameterized decaying float.

entries.query: What does 'authority-by-volatility' mean for how the recall payload should present durable vs. volatile memory?
entries.rationale: Tension #6's resolution: durable conventions read as authoritative, volatile facts read as verify-first — a projection of V6-1's per-note tier/status field, not a separate mechanism.

entries.query: Why is Tencent's DB-Agent-Memory considered the most useful of the MemoryOS-family scans for the planned V6 build?
entries.rationale: The scan's addendum calls it 'the most useful for our planned build — a concrete DB-memory impl with published benchmarks,' unlike the community memory-os project which had none at scan time.

entries.query: What does the vault-obsidian how-to require as a prerequisite before setting up Obsidian on the vault?
entries.rationale: States the vault must already be backed by a transport — vault-git or vault-drive — before Obsidian is layered on top.
